acceptance_criteria.description: ConceptGraph::from_describe_model(json: &str) parses a describe_model JSON fixture and returns a graph where node count matches the number of measures + dimension levels + calcs + hierarchies in the fixture.

Edges typed aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, and derives_from are present in the graph for fixtures that contain the corresponding semantic relationships; each edge carries a typed enum variant, not a raw string.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs: assert edge set contains each EdgeKind variant present in fixture

acceptance_criteria.description: from_describe_model returns Err (not panic) when given invalid or empty JSON.
Graph traversal: given a node id, neighbors(id, EdgeKind) returns only edges of the specified kind, with correct source and target node ids.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs: neighbors filtered by each EdgeKind variant match expected adjacency from fixture

acceptance_criteria.description: from_describe_model completes in under 50 ms for a describe_model fixture containing 500 measures and 2000 dimension levels on the harness machine.
All public types implement Debug and have doc-comments explaining their semantics.
acceptance_criteria.test: cargo doc --no-deps --deny warnings exits 0; cargo test --doc passes

acceptance_criteria.description: ConceptGraph serializes to and deserializes from a stable JSON representation for future model-graph-diff use.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs: roundtrip serialize->deserialize produces identical node/edge sets

ambiguities_resolved.question: Vision lists 5 edge types including 'related_to' but the Gen-8 context names only 4: aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from. Which are in scope?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: All 5 are in scope for the EdgeKind enum (related_to is a catch-all for future edges); AC2 tests only the 4 primary typed edges that carry specific semantic meaning, as those are the ones downstream consumers depend on.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the lib expose a mutable graph (add/remove nodes post-construction) or immutable (build-once, read-many)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Immutable for v1 — from_describe_model returns a fully-built read-only graph. Mutation is not required by any Gen-8 consumer and complicates the borrow model unnecessarily.

five_whys_trace.a: Because every downstream Gen-8 tool (causal-tracer, hallucination-guard, gap-miner, nl-model-extender) re-extracts its own subset of the semantic model topology ad hoc from the same JSON, causing duplication and divergence.
five_whys_trace.q: Why build a concept graph library from describe_model output?

five_whys_trace.a: Because the graph topology is the structural truth of the semantic model — if two tools extract it differently they will reason inconsistently about the same model, undermining the governance guarantees AtScale provides.
five_whys_trace.q: Why is ad-hoc per-tool extraction a problem?

five_whys_trace.a: Because the causal tracer must traverse exactly the same derivation paths the query planner would follow; any missing or mis-typed edge (e.g. aggregates_via vs derives_from) produces a causal explanation that contradicts the actual query result — a compliance risk in regulated industries.
five_whys_trace.q: Why do governance guarantees depend on consistent graph topology?

five_whys_trace.a: There are already 4 downstream consumers identified in the Gen-8 vision and the count will grow; fixing each in isolation is O(n) rework with no shared contract, and any future model schema change would require fixing every consumer again. A single lib gives one change point.
five_whys_trace.q: Why not fix each downstream tool's extraction individually instead of building a shared lib?

additional.no_external_db: graph is in-memory; no rocksdb/sled/sqlite dependency allowed
additional.no_llm_calls: all graph construction must be deterministic from JSON structure alone

non_goals: No persistence to disk or external graph database (v1 is in-memory only).
No LLM calls — the graph is derived entirely from the structural JSON, deterministically.
No causal inference or statistical reasoning — this lib only materializes the structural topology; interpretation belongs to mcp-causal-tracer.
No query execution against AtScale clusters — this is a pure data-structure crate.
No SML YAML generation or model extension — that belongs to mcp-nl-model-extender.
No binary / CLI entry point — target_kind is lib; callers link against it.
root_motivation: Make the AtScale semantic model's graph topology an explicit, shared, single-source-of-truth artifact — derived from describe_model JSON in-process without LLM calls — so that all Gen-8 downstream tools (causal-tracer, narrative-builder, nl-model-extender) consume a consistent attributed property graph rather than each re-extracting topology ad hoc.
scope: Parse describe_model JSON into an in-memory attributed property graph (adjacency list).
Represent nodes as typed enum: Measure, DimensionLevel, Calc, Hierarchy.
Represent edges as typed enum: aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from, related_to.
Expose graph traversal API: node lookup by id, neighbor iteration filtered by EdgeKind.
Return structured errors (not panics) for malformed input.
No external database or file I/O — pure in-process construction from a JSON string.
user_persona: Rust library consumer (another AtScale crate, e.g. mcp-causal-tracer or mcp-nl-model-extender) that calls ConceptGraph::from_describe_model(json) and traverses nodes and typed edges programmatically in-process.
